narrative:
Julián Andrés Quiñones is Colombia's primary Centre-Forward, holding an impressive overall rating of 7.59/10. This outstanding score places him in the top 1% of all centre-forwards participating in this tournament. His current rating, which ranges from 7.2 to 7.97, largely stems from his club form, with 82% from the last two seasons.

Quiñones demonstrates exceptional attacking efficiency, scoring 0.87 goals per 90 minutes at this World Cup against an expected goals (xG) of just 0.35 per 90 minutes. He also contributes significantly to build-up play, registering 0.217 assists per 90 minutes and delivering 2.174 key passes per 90 minutes. Despite his club xG per 90 min being 0.825, his World Cup output highlights a remarkable ability to convert chances.

Quiñones clearly plays a critical role as both a finisher and a creator for his national team. Scouts and fans should continue to monitor his extraordinary goal-scoring efficiency, especially how consistently he outperforms his expected goals. His high overall rating and prolific output make him a crucial figure for Colombia in their remaining fixtures.
